title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Cacciatore Recipe | Pollo alla Cacciatora
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Chicken Cacciatore with our easy Pollo alla Cacciatora recipe. This classic Italian hunter-style chicken is rich, flavorful, and perfect for dinner.
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Hunter Style Chicken
Tips for the Best Chicken Cacciatore
seo.imageAlt: A rustic plate of authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Italy with this hearty and flavorful homemade Chicken Cacciatore, perfect for any night.

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora, or Hunter Style Chicken, is a rustic Italian classic featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory sauce with tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, and aromatic herbs. This hearty dish embodies the warmth of Italian home cooking, perfect for a comforting meal that transports you straight to the Italian countryside.

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels to ensure a good sear. Season generously all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Place the chicken pieces in the hot oil, skin-side down, in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Brown until golden and crispy on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the browned chicken and set aside on a plate.

instructions.title: Sauté the Vegetables
instructions.text: In the same pot with the remaining drippings, add the chopped onion, sliced bell peppers, and sliced mushrooms. Sauté over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables have softened. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Add Tomatoes
instructions.text: Pour in the dry white wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon. Let the wine simmer for 2-3 minutes until it has reduced by about half. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, fresh rosemary sprigs, dried thyme, and bay leaf. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Braise the Chicken
instructions.text: Carefully return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the simmering sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged in the liquid. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is fork-tender and cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the chicken is cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Discard the rosemary sprigs and bay leaf. Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley before serving. Serve hot with your favorite side.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, marinate the chicken in a little olive oil, garlic, and herbs for at least 30 minutes before browning.
Do not overcrowd the pot when browning the chicken; work in batches to ensure a good sear, which locks in flavor and creates a richer sauce.
This dish tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken is a beloved Italian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
